metadata.name: SBP ETGRMF
metadata.description: The Enterprise Technology Governance and Risk Management Framework (ETGRMF) is a mandatory regulatory framework issued by the State Bank of Pakistan (SBP) for financial institutions to ensure robust IT governance, information security, and operational resilience.
metadata.classification_reasoning: The SBP ETGRMF is a mandatory regulatory framework issued by the State Bank of Pakistan for financial institutions operating under its jurisdiction.
metadata.framework_purpose: To establish comprehensive technology governance and risk management requirements for financial institutions in Pakistan.
metadata.framework_scope: All financial institutions regulated by the State Bank of Pakistan, including banks, DFIs, microfinance banks, and other entities under SBP's supervisory purview.
framework_objectives: Establish effective IT governance structures aligned with business objectives
Ensure robust IT service delivery and operations management
Manage technology acquisition and implementation risks
Maintain business continuity and disaster recovery capabilities
Enable effective IT audit and oversight mechanisms
metadata.target_audience: CIOs, CISOs, IT Governance Teams, Compliance Officers, Internal Auditors, and Board Members of SBP-regulated financial institutions
metadata.penalty_for_non_compliance: Non-compliance may result in regulatory penalties, restrictions, and enforcement actions as per SBP regulations and the Banking Companies Ordinance.
adoption_approach: Review current IT governance and security practices against framework requirements
Conduct gap assessment across all six framework domains
Develop implementation roadmap with prioritized action items
Implement required controls and governance structures
Establish ongoing monitoring and reporting mechanisms
Submit compliance reports to SBP as required

controls.title: Board responsibility to establish technology governance framework
controls.description: The Board must create a governance framework to ensure technology aligns with and supports the organization's goals.
controls.full_text: The Board of Directors of the FI(s) are responsible to establish a comprehensive enterprise technology governance framework which defines the leadership, organizational structures and processes to ensure that the FI(s)' technology sustains and extends the enterprise's strategies and objectives.
controls.ai_notes: Clear directive for the Board to establish a governance framework.

controls.title: Evaluate technology use and direct implementation
controls.description: Ensure that plans and policies are created and implemented to align technology usage with business goals.
controls.full_text: The primary objective of the technology governance framework is to evaluate the current and future use of technology, direct the preparation and implementation of plans and policies to ensure that use of technology meets business objectives and monitor compliance to policies and performance against the plans.
controls.ai_notes: Directive to ensure alignment of technology plans with business objectives.

controls.title: Asset classification and control
controls.description: FIs shall maintain an inventory of all information assets, identify information owners responsible for confidentiality, integrity and protection. Management shall implement an information classification strategy and define controls per classification. Establish secure processes for disposal and destruction of sensitive information.
controls.full_text: The FI(s) shall maintain an inventory of all information assets and identify the information owners, who shall be responsible to ensure confidentiality, integrity and protection of these assets. Further, the management shall implement an information classification strategy in accordance with the degree of sensitivity and criticality of information assets. Moreover, the FI(s) shall develop guidelines and definitions for each classification and define an appropriate set of controls and procedures for information protection in accordance with the classification scheme. In addition, the FI(s) shall establish secure processes for disposal and destruction of sensitive information in both paper and electronic media.
controls.ai_notes: Comprehensive asset classification and control requirements.

evidence_requirements.description: Comprehensive inventory of all information assets with identified owners

evidence_requirements.description: Policy defining classification levels, guidelines and controls for each classification

evidence_requirements.title: Disposal and Destruction Procedures

controls.title: Physical and environmental protection
controls.description: Physical security measures shall protect IT facilities. Critical processing facilities must be in secure areas with access restricted to authorized personnel and reviewed regularly. Environmental threats must be considered for data center locations. Environmental controls shall monitor conditions. Equipment must be protected from power failures.
controls.full_text: Physical security measures shall be in place to protect IT facilities and equipment from damage or unauthorized access. Critical information processing facilities shall be housed in secure areas such as data centers and network equipment rooms with appropriate security barriers and entry controls. Access to these areas shall be restricted to authorized personnel only and the access rights shall be reviewed and updated regularly. The FI(s) shall consider environmental threats when selecting the locations of data centers. Further, physical and environmental controls shall be implemented to monitor environmental conditions which can adversely affect the operation of information processing facilities. Moreover, FI(s) shall take adequate measures to protect equipment from power failures and electrical supply fluctuations.
controls.ai_notes: Comprehensive physical and environmental protection requirements.

evidence_requirements.title: Physical Security Policy
evidence_requirements.description: Policy and procedures for physical security of IT facilities

evidence_requirements.title: Access Control Records
evidence_requirements.description: Records of access controls to secure areas and regular access rights reviews

evidence_requirements.title: Environmental Controls Documentation
evidence_requirements.description: Documentation of environmental controls and power protection measures

controls.title: Security administration and monitoring
controls.description: FIs shall put in place a security administration function with formal procedures for administering access rights and monitoring system resources. The least privilege principle shall be employed throughout IT operations. Systems and security administrators shall have no transactional authority.
controls.full_text: The FI(s) shall put in place a security administration function and set formal procedures for administering the allocation of access rights to system resources and application systems and monitoring the use of system resources to detect any unusual or unauthorized activities. Further, FI(s) shall employ the "least privilege" principle throughout IT operations. Moreover, individuals with systems and security administrator roles and privileges shall have no transactional authority.
controls.ai_notes: Security administration, least privilege, and segregation of duties requirements.

evidence_requirements.title: Security Administration Procedures
evidence_requirements.description: Formal procedures for administering access rights and monitoring system resources

evidence_requirements.title: Access Rights Records
evidence_requirements.description: Records of access rights allocations following least privilege principle

evidence_requirements.title: Segregation of Duties Evidence
evidence_requirements.description: Evidence that systems/security administrators have no transactional authority

controls.title: Authentication and access control
controls.description: FIs shall have an effective process to manage user authentication and access control. Authentication mechanisms shall be commensurate with information classification. Users must accept/acknowledge an Acceptable-Use Policy before using systems. Staff with security management functions shall not perform other duties creating conflict of interest.
controls.full_text: The FI(s) shall have an effective process to manage user authentication and access control. For this purpose, appropriate user authentication mechanism commensurate with the classification of information to be accessed shall be selected. Users, who can access internal systems, shall be required to accept/acknowledge an Acceptable-Use Policy (AUP) document before using a system. Staff assigned a security management function shall not perform other duties which can create any conflict of interest.
controls.ai_notes: Authentication, AUP acknowledgement, and conflict of interest requirements.

evidence_requirements.title: Authentication Policy and Procedures
evidence_requirements.description: Policy and procedures for user authentication and access control

controls.title: Report cyber security breaches to BPRD within 48 hours
controls.description: FIs shall report to BPRD, SBP within 48 hours all established information/cyber security breaches involving financial loss, stealing of confidential data, or major disruption resulting in non-availability of banking services for customers for more than two hours.
controls.full_text: The FI(s) shall report to Banking Policy & Regulation Department (BPRD), SBP within forty-eight (48) hours after the incident all established information/cyber security breaches and related incidents involving financial loss, stealing of confidential data and major disruption in the banking system or communication channel resulting in non availability of banking services for customers for more than two (02) hours.
controls.ai_notes: 48-hour reporting deadline; triggers include 2-hour service unavailability.

controls.title: Establish comprehensive testing program for information security
controls.description: FIs shall establish a comprehensive testing program to validate information security environment effectiveness. Tests shall include internal and external stakeholders where applicable. Production data must be properly masked in test environments involving external parties.
controls.full_text: The FI(s) shall establish a comprehensive testing program to validate the effectiveness of its information security environment on a regular basis. The results of the testing program shall be used by the FI(s) to support the improvement in their Information/cyber security. Where applicable, these tests shall include both internal and external stakeholders such as business line management, incident & crisis response teams and any other relevant departments/functions. Further, the FI(s) shall also ensure that the all production data is properly masked in the test environment involving any external party, however, for internal testing purposes, appropriate data masking may be done based on the criticality of data.
controls.ai_notes: Comprehensive testing program with data masking requirements.

controls.full_text: FI(s) shall perform vulnerability assessments to identify and assess security vulnerabilities in their systems and processes. The FI(s) shall also perform subsequent validation tests to assess that the gaps identified during VA have been properly filled in.
controls.ai_notes: Both vulnerability assessment and subsequent validation testing are required.

controls.title: Conduct scenario-based testing for response and recovery plans
controls.description: FIs' response, resumption and recovery plans shall be subject to periodic review and testing using broad scenarios including simulation of extreme but plausible cyber attacks, challenging assumptions of response practices.
controls.full_text: FI(s)' response, resumption and recovery plans shall be subject to periodic review and testing. Tests shall address an appropriately broad scope of scenarios, including simulation of extreme but plausible cyber attacks. Further, the tests shall be designed to challenge the assumptions of response, resumption and recovery practices including governance arrangements and communication plans. FI(s) shall also conduct exercises to test the ability of their staff and processes to respond to unfamiliar scenarios, with a view to achieve strong operational resilience.
controls.ai_notes: Scenario-based testing including extreme cyber attack simulations.

controls.full_text: FI(s) shall carry out periodic penetration tests to identify vulnerabilities that may affect their systems, networks, people or processes. Penetration tests on internal systems shall also be conducted at the time of major update and deployment of the software/system.

FIs shall update the risk register periodically and develop a monitoring and review process for continuous assessment and treatment of risks.
controls.full_text: The FI(s) shall maintain a risk register to facilitate the monitoring and reporting of risks by prioritizing and closely monitoring high risk activities with regular reporting on the actions that have been taken to mitigate them. Further, the FI(s) shall update the risk register periodically and develop a monitoring and review process for continuous assessment and treatment of risks.

controls.title: Establish patch management procedures
controls.description: FIs shall establish procedures to test patches in a segregated environment and install them when appropriate. The procedures shall include identification, categorization, prioritization of security patches and their testing processes.
controls.full_text: Patch Management is a practice designed to proactively prevent the exploitation of vulnerabilities on IT devices. The FI(s) shall establish procedures to test patches in a segregated environment, and to install them when appropriate. The procedures shall include the identification, categorization, prioritization of security patches and their testing processes.
controls.ai_notes: Comprehensive patch management procedures with segregated testing are mandatory.

evidence_requirements.description: Documented patch management procedures including identification, categorization, prioritization and testing

evidence_requirements.title: Patch Deployment Records
evidence_requirements.description: Records of patch deployments following approved testing

controls.title: Initiate capacity planning for internal and external factors
controls.description: FIs shall initiate capacity planning to address internal factors (growth, mergers, acquisitions, new product lines, new technologies) and external factors (shift in customer preferences, competitor capability, regulatory or market requirements).
controls.full_text: The FI(s) shall initiate capacity planning to address internal factors (growth, mergers, acquisitions, new product lines and the implementation of new technologies) and external factors (shift in customer preferences, competitor capability or regulatory or market requirements).
controls.ai_notes: Capacity planning addressing both internal and external factors is mandatory.

evidence_requirements.description: Capacity plans addressing internal and external factors

evidence_requirements.title: Capacity Monitoring Records
evidence_requirements.description: Records of capacity monitoring and planning activities

evidence_requirements.title: Growth Forecasts
evidence_requirements.description: Documentation of growth forecasts used in capacity planning

controls.title: Monitor technology resources for capacity planning
controls.description: FIs shall monitor technology resources for capacity planning including platform processing speed, core storage for each platform's central processing unit, data storage, and voice/data communication bandwidth.
controls.full_text: The FI(s) shall monitor technology resources for capacity planning including platform processing speed, core storage for each platform's central processing unit, data storage, and voice/data communication bandwidth.
controls.ai_notes: Monitoring of specific technology resources for capacity planning is mandatory.

evidence_requirements.description: Defined capacity thresholds and alerts for resource monitoring

controls.title: Integrate capacity planning with budgeting and strategic planning
controls.description: Capacity planning shall be closely integrated with the budgeting and strategic planning processes, and shall address personnel issues including staff size, appropriate training and staff succession plans.
controls.full_text: Capacity planning shall be closely integrated with the budgeting and strategic planning processes. It shall also address personnel issues including staff size, appropriate training and staff succession plans.
controls.ai_notes: Integration with budgeting and strategic planning including personnel capacity is required.

controls.title: Formulate data center procedures per international standards
controls.description: FIs shall formulate procedures in line with best international standards for building and maintaining data center structures and operations.
controls.full_text: The FI(s) shall formulate procedures in line with best international standards for building and maintaining data center structures and operations.
controls.ai_notes: Data center procedures aligned with international standards are mandatory.

evidence_requirements.title: Standards Compliance Evidence
evidence_requirements.description: Evidence of compliance with referenced international data center standards

evidence_requirements.description: Records of data center audits or assessments against standards

controls.title: Create user help desk with KPIs
controls.description: FIs may create users' help desk to ensure efficient job performance; help desk may record and track incoming problem reports; FIs may also define KPIs for the resolution of different problems/issues.
controls.full_text: The FI(s) may create users' help desk to ensure that they perform their job functions in an efficient and effective manner. The Help Desk may record and track incoming problem reports, being handled by live operators or automated systems. Further, FI(s) may also define Key performance indicators (KPI) for the resolution of different problems / issues.
controls.ai_notes: Optional - use of 'may' indicates discretionary action.

evidence_requirements.description: Documentation of help desk structure and processes

evidence_requirements.title: Problem Tracking Records
evidence_requirements.description: Records of incoming problem reports tracked through help desk

evidence_requirements.title: Help Desk KPIs

controls.title: Establish technology project management framework
controls.description: FIs shall establish a framework for management of major technology-related projects specifying the project management methodology. The methodology shall at a minimum cover structure, roles and responsibilities, activity breakdown, budgeting, milestones, check points, key dependencies, quality assurance, change management, risk assessment and approvals.
controls.full_text: Establish a framework for management of major technology-related projects. This framework shall, among other things, specify the project management methodology to be adopted. The methodology shall at a minimum cover structure, roles and responsibilities of the staff, activity breakdown, budgeting of time and resources, milestones, check points, key dependencies, quality assurance, change management, risk assessment and approvals.
controls.ai_notes: Comprehensive project management framework with defined methodology components is mandatory.

evidence_requirements.title: Project Management Framework Document
evidence_requirements.description: Documented project management framework specifying methodology with all required components

evidence_requirements.title: Framework Approval
evidence_requirements.description: Management approval of project management framework

evidence_requirements.title: Framework Application Records
evidence_requirements.description: Evidence of framework application to technology projects

controls.title: Establish Project Management Office (PMO)
controls.description: FIs shall establish a PMO to promote sound management practices based on project size and complexity, responsible for centralized project records, monitoring and reporting to IT Steering Committee on a periodical basis.
controls.full_text: Establish a Project Management Office (PMO) to promote sound management practices and principles based on the size and complexity of technology-related projects. Further, PMO shall be responsible to maintain centralized record of all technology related projects, monitor and report the status of these projects to the IT steering committee on periodical basis.
controls.ai_notes: Mandatory PMO establishment for centralized project management.

evidence_requirements.title: PMO Charter
evidence_requirements.description: PMO charter defining scope, responsibilities and reporting structure

evidence_requirements.title: Project Registry
evidence_requirements.description: Centralized registry of all technology-related projects maintained by PMO

evidence_requirements.title: PMO Reports to IT Steering Committee
evidence_requirements.description: Periodic project status reports submitted by PMO to IT Steering Committee

controls.title: Adopt full project life cycle methodology
controls.description: FIs shall adopt and implement a full project life cycle methodology governing major computer systems, covering phases of project initiation, feasibility study, business requirement definition, system design, program development, system and acceptance testing, training, implementation, operation and maintenance. The methodology shall include roles and deliverables from each phase.
controls.full_text: Adopt and implement a full project life cycle methodology governing the process of developing, implementing and maintaining major computer systems. In general, this shall involve phases of project initiation, feasibility study, business requirement definition, system design, program development, system and acceptance testing, training, implementation, operation and maintenance. The project life cycle methodology shall include roles and responsibilities for the project team and the deliverables from each phase.
controls.ai_notes: Full SDLC methodology with all specified phases is mandatory.

evidence_requirements.title: Project Life Cycle Methodology Document
evidence_requirements.description: Documented full project life cycle methodology covering all required phases

evidence_requirements.title: Phase Deliverables Documentation
evidence_requirements.description: Documentation of deliverables and roles for each project lifecycle phase

evidence_requirements.title: Methodology Application Evidence
evidence_requirements.description: Evidence of methodology application to actual projects

controls.title: Engage independent party for QA review of major technology projects
controls.description: FIs shall engage an independent party to conduct a quality assurance review of major technology projects after every five years.
controls.full_text: Engage an independent party to conduct a quality assurance review of major technology projects after every five (05) years.
controls.ai_notes: Independent QA review of major technology projects every 5 years is mandatory.

evidence_requirements.title: Engagement Records

controls.full_text: The FI(s) shall assess and mitigate operational risks associated with the development or acquisition of software by using a Secure System Development Life Cycle (SDLC) or similar methodology appropriate for their specific technology environment.

controls.description: The technology procurement policy shall include types of technology assets for both hardware and software, types of vendors, selection criteria, acquisition process, payment procedures and monitoring, delivery assurance and verification process, technical vetting requirement and need assessment.
controls.full_text: The technology procurement policy shall also include types of technology assets for both hardware & software, types of vendors for hardware & software, selection criteria for vendors, acquisition process, payment procedures & monitoring, delivery assurance & verification process, technical vetting requirement and need assessment.

controls.title: Identify and record requirements in RFP/ITT
controls.description: FIs shall ensure that functional, operational and regulatory requirements are identified and recorded in RFPs or ITTs in the bid solicitation process. Public sector FIs shall also follow PPRA rules.
controls.full_text: FI(s) shall ensure that functional, operational and regulatory requirements are identified and recorded in Request-For-Proposals (RFP) or Invitations-To-Tender (ITT) in the bid solicitation process. Public sector FI(s) shall also follow PPRA rules for procurement of any software or hardware solutions or consultancy services.
controls.ai_notes: Public sector FIs must also follow PPRA rules.

controls.title: Review RFP and BRD for alignment with IT strategy
controls.description: The RFP and BRD for any new technology acquisition shall be reviewed to ascertain comprehensiveness, alignment with IT strategy and business objectives, and technical alignments with emerging trends and technologies.
controls.full_text: The RFP and BRD for any new technology acquisition shall be reviewed to ascertain comprehensiveness of the documents, their alignment with IT strategy & business objectives and technical alignments with emerging trends and technologies.
controls.ai_notes: Clear directive to review RFP and BRD for alignment.

evidence_requirements.description: Records of reviews confirming alignment with IT strategy and business objectives

controls.full_text: FI(s) may obtain expert consultancy/ advisory services for the formulation of RFP/BRD/Procurement process, where it deems necessary, provided they record reasons and rationale for the same.
controls.ai_notes: Optional; evidence requirements focus on documentation of rationale.

controls.description: FIs shall conduct a system (hardware, software and services) selection analysis to ensure that user and business requirements are met, expected service levels are properly achieved as per contract agreements and all applicable legal/regulatory requirements are complied with.
controls.full_text: FI(s) shall conduct a system (hardware, software and services) selection analysis to ensure that user and business requirements are met, expected service levels are properly achieved as per contract agreements and all applicable legal/regulatory requirements are complied with.
controls.ai_notes: Mandatory system selection analysis for all hardware, software and services.

controls.description: FIs shall undergo a transparent and competitive process of acquisitions in major procurements. Whenever signing up for direct contracting is required, it should be fairly justified based on the requirements.
controls.full_text: FI(s) shall undergo a transparent and competitive process of acquisitions in major procurements. Whenever signing up for direct contracting is required, it should be fairly justified based on the requirements (such as technical grounds, urgency and other matters).
controls.ai_notes: Direct contracting must be justified when competitive process is not followed.

controls.title: Manage procurement by independent unit and IT Steering Committee

controls.full_text: The procurement process shall be managed by an independent unit and governed by IT Steering Committee for different scope / level / size of procurements.

controls.full_text: FI(s) shall also ensure that vendors deliver hardware/ software as per terms and conditions set in the contract/agreement. The delivery of technology assets shall be assessed against purchase orders, prepared in light of RFPs/ BRDs, and the same shall be signed off by the respective departments.

controls.full_text: FI(s) shall ensure that only properly tested and approved systems are promoted to the production environment.

controls.full_text: Carry out system and User Acceptance Testing (UAT) in an environment separate from the production environment.

controls.full_text: Ensure that production data is not used in development or acceptance testing unless the data has been desensitized and prior approval from the information owner has been obtained.
controls.ai_notes: Data desensitization and information owner approval are required before using production data in testing.

controls.full_text: Carry out performance testing of newly developed critical systems to ensure effective and smooth operation before deploying the same in production environment.
controls.ai_notes: Performance testing of critical systems before production deployment is mandatory.

controls.full_text: Conduct system testing using documented test plans encompassing all predetermined data or processing problems and business scenarios.

controls.full_text: Define roles and responsibilities of officers to ensure that all changes to system, application and configuration documentation are made according to prescribed standards.

controls.full_text: Control access to documentation libraries with appropriate library and version controls.

controls.title: Ensure complete and secure system documentation
controls.description: FIs shall ensure that complete and updated system documentation of applications is available and secured against unauthorized access.
controls.full_text: Ensure that complete and updated system documentation of such applications is available and are secured against unauthorized access.
controls.ai_notes: Complete, updated and secured documentation is mandatory.

evidence_requirements.description: Records of periodic documentation completeness reviews

controls.title: Establish change management process for production systems
controls.description: FIs shall establish a change management process to ensure that changes to production systems are approved, implemented and reviewed in a controlled manner.
controls.full_text: FI(s) shall establish a change management process to ensure that changes to production systems are approved, implemented and reviewed in a controlled manner.

controls.description: The change management process shall apply to changes pertaining to system and security configurations, patches for hardware devices and software updates.
controls.full_text: The change management process shall apply to changes pertaining to system and security configurations, patches for hardware devices and software updates.
controls.ai_notes: Change management scope covers configurations, patches and software updates.

controls.description: FIs shall perform a risk and impact analysis of change requests in relation to existing infrastructure, network, up-stream and downstream systems.
controls.full_text: FI(s) shall perform a risk and impact analysis of the change request in relation to existing infrastructure, network, up-stream and downstream systems.
controls.ai_notes: Risk and impact analysis is mandatory before implementing changes.

controls.title: Test changes and obtain user acceptance before production migration
controls.description: FIs shall adequately test impending changes and ensure user acceptance prior to migration. FIs shall develop and document appropriate test plans and obtain test results with user sign-offs prior to the migration.
controls.full_text: FI(s) shall adequately test the impending change and ensure that it is accepted by users prior to the migration of the changed modules to the production system. The FI(s) shall develop and document appropriate test plans for the impending change. Further, they shall obtain test results with user sign-offs prior to the migration.
controls.ai_notes: Testing with documented plans and user sign-offs are mandatory before production migration.

controls.description: FIs shall establish a rollback plan to revert to a former version if a problem is encountered during or after deployment, and alternative recovery options where rollback is not possible.
controls.full_text: FI(s) shall establish a rollback plan to revert to a former version of the system or application if a problem is encountered during or after the deployment. The FI(s) shall establish alternative recovery options to address situations where a change does not allow the FI to revert to a prior status.
controls.ai_notes: Rollback plan and alternative recovery options are mandatory.

evidence_requirements.title: Alternative Recovery Options
evidence_requirements.description: Documented alternative recovery options for changes that cannot be rolled back

evidence_requirements.description: Evidence of rollback plan testing

controls.full_text: FI(s) shall ensure that the logging facility is enabled to record activities that are performed during the migration process.
controls.ai_notes: Migration activity logging is mandatory.

controls.full_text: Conduct a post implementation review at the end of a project to validate the application's operational performance.
controls.ai_notes: Post-implementation review for critical projects is mandatory.

controls.full_text: Assess the relative success of the project by comparing planned and actual cost, benefits and completion time.
controls.ai_notes: Planned vs actual comparison is mandatory.

evidence_requirements.description: Records of actual vs planned project costs

controls.full_text: Record reasons in a post implementation evaluation report if the planned objectives do not materialize.

controls.description: FIs shall define the business requirements for functions or activities to be outsourced. All IT activities to be outsourced shall be governed under BPRD Circular No 9 dated 13th July 2007 as amended from time to time.
controls.full_text: The FI(s) shall define the business requirements for the functions or activities to be outsourced. All IT activities to be outsourced shall be governed under the instructions conveyed through BP&RD Circular No 9 dated 13th July, 2007 as amended from time to time.
controls.ai_notes: Outsourcing of IT services must comply with BPRD Circular No 9 of 2007.

controls.title: Data ownership responsibility with CSP
controls.description: FIs shall be ultimately responsible for the security of the data when outsourcing to CSPs.
controls.full_text: Data ownership: The FI(s) shall be ultimate responsible for the security of the data.
controls.ai_notes: FI retains ultimate responsibility for data security in cloud arrangements.

controls.description: The CSP or any other entity shall not have the right to view or access the information being routed or stored with the CSP. The sole discretion of access shall be with the FI.
controls.full_text: Security, Privacy & Confidentiality: The CSP or any other entity shall not have the right to view or access the information being routed or stored with the CSP. The sole discretion of access to all such information/data/records/reports etc shall be with the FI(s).
controls.ai_notes: FI must retain sole discretion over data access in cloud arrangements.

controls.description: A CSP shall provide access of all arrangements to FI and SBP.

controls.ai_notes: CSP must grant audit access to both FI and SBP.

controls.full_text: Business Continuity Plan: FI(s) shall have adequate plan to resume business due to any disruption of services through CSP.
controls.ai_notes: BCP for cloud service disruptions is mandatory.

controls.full_text: Exit Strategy: FI(s) shall put in place adequate procedures to exit from the cloud computing arrangement in case of non compliance of terms and condition of the agreement by CSP. These shall also be made the part of agreement with CSP.
controls.ai_notes: Exit strategy must be documented and included in CSP agreements.

controls.full_text: Given the potential issues related to business operations, confidentiality of customer data and legal/regulatory compliance, FI(s) shall only use cloud services for non-core operations and business support processes (e.g., communication tools, office productivity, collaboration tools, HR-related services, procurement functions, Inventory management etc.)
controls.ai_notes: Core banking must not be placed on cloud; only non-core operations are permissible.

controls.description: For all types of cloud services (SaaS, PaaS, IaaS), CSPs shall be located in Pakistan and all physical servers and services shall reside and operate from Pakistan.
controls.full_text: For all types of cloud services including Software as a Service (SaaS), Platform as a Service (PaaS) and Infrastructure as a Service (IaaS), the CSPs shall be located in Pakistan and all physical servers and services (data centers and allied infrastructure) shall reside and operate from Pakistan.
controls.ai_notes: All cloud infrastructure must be physically located within Pakistan.

evidence_requirements.title: Due Diligence Records
evidence_requirements.description: Due diligence records confirming Pakistan-based operations for all cloud service types

controls.title: Prohibit core banking on cloud
controls.description: Core banking applications/services/operations and business processes used to process and store customer/borrower data/information shall not be placed under cloud-based outsourcing arrangements.
controls.full_text: Core banking applications/services/operations and business processes used to process and store customer/borrower data/information shall not be placed under cloud-based outsourcing arrangements.
controls.ai_notes: Absolute prohibition on core banking systems being placed on cloud.

evidence_requirements.description: Policy explicitly prohibiting core banking on cloud

evidence_requirements.description: Inventory confirming no core banking applications are on cloud

evidence_requirements.title: Compliance Assessment Records
evidence_requirements.description: Periodic assessments confirming core banking remains off cloud

controls.full_text: The FI(s) shall develop a comprehensive business continuity plan (BCP) as part of the business continuity planning process.
controls.ai_notes: Comprehensive BCP development is mandatory.

controls.full_text: The BCP shall be based on the size and complexity of the FI(s) and shall be consistent with its overall business strategy.
controls.ai_notes: BCP must be proportionate to FI size and aligned with business strategy.

controls.full_text: The goal of the BCP shall be to minimize financial losses to the institution, serve customers with minimal disruptions and mitigate the negative effects of disruptions on business operations.
controls.ai_notes: BCP objectives are explicitly defined.

evidence_requirements.description: BCP with clearly stated objectives covering financial loss minimization, customer service continuity and disruption mitigation

evidence_requirements.title: BCP Test Results

controls.full_text: Keeping in view the size, nature and complexity of business operations and IT systems, FI(s) shall consider developing built-in redundancies to reduce single points of failure which can bring down the entire network.

controls.title: Develop, implement and maintain enterprise-wide written BCP
controls.description: Risk management involves developing, implementing and maintaining a written enterprise-wide BCP based on comprehensive BIA and risk assessment, reviewed and approved by the board at least annually, with conditions for invocation and flexibility to respond to unanticipated threats.
controls.full_text: Risk management is the process of identifying, assessing, and reducing risk to an acceptable level through the development, implementation and maintenance of a written, enterprise-wide BCP. The BCP shall be based on a comprehensive BIA and risk assessment exercise, reviewed and approved by the board at least annually. It shall be documented in a written program and disseminated across the FI(s). The BCP shall, among other things, specify the conditions which shall prompt implementation of the plan and the process for invoking the BCP and immediate steps to be taken during a disruption. The BCP shall be flexible to respond to unanticipated threat scenarios and changing internal conditions; focused on the impact of various threats that could potentially disrupt operations rather than on specific events; BCP shall be effective in minimizing service disruptions and financial loss through the implementation of mitigation strategies.
controls.ai_notes: Enterprise-wide written BCP with annual board review is mandatory.
